name: security-sentinel
description: Use when working with authentication, API routes, user input, or sensitive data. Audits code for security vulnerabilities based on OWASP Top 10. Critical for payment processing, auth systems, and data handling.
qualityReason: Critical use cases with clear triggers
capabilities: Provides complete OWASP Top 10 documentation with vulnerable and secure code examples
Includes authentication and authorization patterns for JWT, sessions, OAuth, and MFA
Offers input validation patterns using Zod for forms, files, and API routes
Contains automated Python vulnerability scanner that detects 20+ security issues
Delivers pre-deployment security checklist with 250+ audit items
Covers CSRF prevention, rate limiting, and secret management best practices
author: Barnhardt-Enterprises-Inc
securitySummary: This is a legitimate security education and documentation skill. All 1267 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES - the files contain educational code examples showing vulnerable patterns (marked as 'DON'T') alongside secure alternatives. The skill includes a defensive security scanner for detecting vulnerabilities. No malicious intent found.
valueStatement: Build secure applications by following OWASP Top 10 guidelines. This comprehensive reference includes authentication patterns, vulnerability prevention guides, and automated security scanning for your codebase.
userTitle: Secure Your Next.js 15 Apps with OWASP Best Practices
limitations: Scanner requires Python 3 and does not run inside the AI execution environment
Does not automatically fix vulnerabilities - provides guidance for manual remediation
Security patterns are documentation-based; actual implementation requires developer action

useCases.title: Secure API implementation
useCases.description: Implement authentication and authorization in Next.js 15 API routes following OWASP guidelines

useCases.title: Code review automation
useCases.description: Run automated security scans on codebases to identify vulnerabilities before deployment

useCases.title: Pre-deployment security audit
useCases.description: Validate all security requirements are met using comprehensive checklist before production release

promptTemplates.title: Auth implementation
promptTemplates.scenario: Add user authentication to Next.js app
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to implement secure JWT authentication in Next.js 15 with proper token refresh, HttpOnly cookies, and authorization middleware

promptTemplates.title: Input validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Zod schema to validate user registration input including email format, password strength requirements, and prevent SQL injection

promptTemplates.title: XSS prevention
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I safely render user-generated HTML in Next.js without XSS vulnerabilities? Show DOMPurify integration and Content Security Policy headers

promptTemplates.title: Security audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Audit this code for OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities. Check for broken access control, injection risks, and missing security headers

bestPractices: Validate all user input with Zod schemas before processing - never trust client data
Use parameterized queries only - never concatenate strings in database operations
Hash passwords with bcrypt (12+ rounds) or Argon2, and store JWT secrets in environment variables
antiPatterns: Using eval() or Function() with user input - leads to code execution vulnerabilities
Storing secrets in source code or hardcoded strings - use environment variables only
Returning sensitive data (passwords, tokens) in API responses - always filter response objects

faq.question: Does this skill run security scans automatically?
faq.answer: The skill provides a Python scanner script. Run it separately with 'python validate-security.py src/' to scan your codebase.

faq.question: What authentication methods are covered?
faq.answer: JWT tokens, session cookies, OAuth 2.0, magic links, TOTP MFA, and passwordless authentication patterns.

faq.question: Can this prevent all security vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: No tool guarantees complete security. This skill provides patterns and checks - developers must implement them correctly.

faq.question: Is this compatible with Next.js 15?
faq.answer: Yes, all examples use App Router patterns, Server Actions, and modern Next.js 15 security features.

faq.question: Does the scanner check TypeScript files?
faq.answer: Yes, the Python scanner detects vulnerabilities in .ts, .tsx, .js, and .jsx files while excluding node_modules.

faq.question: How often should I run security checks?
faq.answer: Run security scans before every commit and deployment. Use the checklist for final pre-release validation.
